text.title: Agent Eval Test-Set Curation Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-eval-test-set-curation enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Golden set has ≥30 trajectories; ≥30% sourced from real production traces (not synthetic).
rule.rationale: Synthetic-only sets miss the long tail; <30 examples lack statistical power for drift detection.

rule.statement: Each trajectory has ≥3 tags: capability (e.g., 'tool-use', 'retrieval'), persona (e.g., 'novice', 'expert'), difficulty (1-5).
rule.rationale: Untagged sets cannot diagnose which capability regressed; harness needs tags to slice subsets.

rule.statement: Every real-trace-sourced trajectory carries a `provenance` field with trace ID, sampling rule, capture date.
rule.rationale: Without provenance, the set drifts and is impossible to refresh from fresh production data.

rule.statement: Golden set is version-controlled at `evals/golden.jsonl`; changes go through PR with reviewer.
rule.rationale: Drift in the eval set is invisible from scores; PR review is the only forcing function.

rule.statement: Set is partitioned: fast-subset tag for ≤20% of cases (stratified across capabilities); full-suite is the remainder.
rule.rationale: Without partition, harness has to manually sample for PR cadence each time.
